commentary: Okay, four activities versus fourteen last week — babes, I love you, but that is a dramatic drop and we are both going to acknowledge it. That said, what you did do tells a story: you doubled your Tuesday swim from the week before, going from a hard 2000m to a cleaner, stronger 1500m at Prince Alfred with a heart rate that actually suggests you were working rather than surviving, which is not nothing. The Mosman circuit held its own at 13.3km and your heart rate stayed controlled compared to last Saturday's 149, so the fitness is quietly bedding in even when the volume falls off a cliff. Rest is a thing, recovery is a thing, but fourteen to four is giving less "intentional deload" and more "the week got away from you," so this week we are putting the jockstrap back on and getting back in the pool, yes?
metrics_commentary: Okay, 13.3 kilometres in a single session and 2250 metres in the pool — that's not a quiet week, that's a statement, and the body is clearly keeping up with it because that HRV of 62 is sitting comfortably above the seven-day average of 51, which means recovery is actually trending in the right direction rather than just surviving. The VO2 Max at 56.7 is genuinely strong, the kind of number that says the engine is built, not borrowed. The only thing worth watching is keeping that session count from bottoming out — one run across the whole week is fine until it becomes a habit, and a body this capable deserves more chances to show off.
gym_commentary: One session out of four, babe — we are not going to pretend that's not a gap, because it is, and Scott's programme deserves more than a cameo appearance. Volume dropped from 24,518 kg down to 20,148 kg, which tracks when you've only been in the room once, and there are no new PRs to report this week. What you do still have is a trap bar deadlift sitting at 107.9 kg, a leg press at 168.0 kg, and a body that knows exactly what it's capable of when it shows up. One session is not a spiral, it's just a week that got away from you — now go put on something that shows off the work and get back in there.
